name: Andrew Hill
abstract:
Andrew Hill (June 30, 1931 – April 20, 2007) was an American jazz pianist and composer. Jazz critic John Fordham described Hill as a "uniquely gifted composer, pianist and educator" although "his status remained largely inside knowledge in the jazz world for most of his career."
Hill recorded for Blue Note Records for nearly a decade, pr…

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: I was grateful to have had an opportunity to basically from time to time perform with Andrew at the beginning of my career you know. I mean right off the bat, 1964 actually you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Tolliver interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: Andrew Hill and I were tight... Andrew had a record day with Blue Note and he wanted me on the date. It was point of departure.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: And I played this festival and this band was Andrew Hill, Bobby Hutcherson, Ron Carter, Roy Hanyes, Joe Henderson and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Right when all that was going on, I’m recording an album with Andrew Hill.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: Andrew Hill. Andrew is very grateful to me and I’m proud of that. Andrew says you stopped writing about me — “All the articles until you wrote about me, Howard, were about me as a performer in the sixties. You brought me up to date.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I found out that Malachi who I knew at the time, was working in with we were going at it as a trio, and Andrew Hill, and he had a fiddle that he wanted to sell
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
